mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative risk sentiment and driving down S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of autos and light trucks and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer durables and housing demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation indexed by CPIAUCSL crosses above 210.0, persistent price pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 210.0 are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rate indicates labor force participation expansion masking underlying weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal shows no predictive power for 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple macro cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects strong aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show negative or zero correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production crosses below 108.0, manufacturing sector contraction triggers supply chain bottlenecks and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below 108.0 are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 58% of triggered instances.
